seoTitle: Sunset vs Sunshine Beach — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunset and Sunshine Beach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sunset vs Sunshine Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunset and Sunshine Beach.
heading: Sunset vs Sunshine Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunset, Queensland and Sunshine Beach, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunset has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Sunshine Beach (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunset or Sunshine Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunset has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Sunshine Beach scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunset scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Sunshine Beach scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunset or Sunshine Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunset has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
